- Monday - Friday 8:30AM - 5:30PM US PST



***TO APPLY***



Please click link and complete.

title: Social Media & Foot Traffic Growth Manager (Food Destination / Tourism)
description:
Job Title

Social Media & Foot Traffic Growth Manager (Food Destination / Tourism)



About Us

We operate a high-traffic outdoor food destination in a major tourist area, home to 20+ food vendors and a popular stop for both locals and visitors.

Our focus is on significantly increasing foot traffic, customer volume, and overall vendor sales through strong digital marketing, high-performing content, and strategic promotion.



Role Overview

We are seeking a highly skilled, results-driven Social Media & Growth Manager who understands how to turn online attention into real-world foot traffic and revenue.



This is not a basic posting role.



We are looking for someone who knows how to:

Create high-performing content

Leverage trends and algorithms across all major social media platforms

Influence real customer behavior and drive in-person visits



Success in this role will be measured by:

Increased visibility

Increased engagement

Increased foot traffic and customer volume



Key Responsibilities

Traffic & Growth Strategy

Develop and execute strategies specifically focused on increasing foot traffic to a physical destination

Identify and implement high-impact marketing opportunities



Content Direction & Execution

Create or direct high-performing short-form content (Reels, TikTok, Shorts, etc.)

Develop strong hooks and concepts that attract both locals and tourists

Multi-Platform Management



Manage and grow presence across Instagram, TikTok,
, YouTube, Google, Yelp, and other relevant platforms

Understand and leverage platform-specific algorithms across all channels to maximize reach and engagement

Ensure consistent, high-quality posting



Influencer & Community Marketing

Identify and collaborate with local influencers, food creators, and travel content creators

Drive user-generated content and organic exposure



Performance Optimization

Track content performance and continuously refine strategy

Focus on what drives real visits and purchasing behavior, not just views



Requirements

Proven experience growing social media accounts across multiple platforms

Strong understanding of algorithms and content distribution across major platforms (Instagram, TikTok,
, YouTube, Google, etc.)

Demonstrated ability to drive traffic to physical businesses (restaurants, retail, tourism, etc.)

Experience with short-form video strategy and content creation

Strategic thinker with strong execution ability

Strong integrity and honesty in reporting time, performance, and completed work

Reliable, efficient, and highly accountable



What We’re Looking For

Someone who understands that views alone are not enough, conversions matter

Someone who has actually influenced real-world customer behavior

Someone proactive, creative, and results-oriented



Time Commitment

This role will initially require a higher time commitment during setup and ramp-up, which may approach full-time hours in the beginning

As systems, content pipelines, and processes are established, the role will transition into a part-time position (approximately 5–20 hours per week)

Flexibility and efficiency are important, with a strong focus on results and execution



Compensation

Hourly rate or monthly retainer based on experience and proven results

Higher compensation available for candidates with strong, relevant track records

Performance-based incentives may be considered based on measurable growth



Work Expectations

This is an output-focused role, not time-based

Tasks are expected to be completed efficiently within a reasonable timeframe

Reported hours must reflect actual productive work only

Integrity and accuracy in time reporting are essential for this role

Regular summaries of completed work and progress are required



Payment Policy

Payments are made on a fixed weekly or biweekly schedule

No salary advances or early payments will be provided

Please apply only if you are comfortable with this structure



Ideal Candidate

Results-driven and performance-focused

Reliable, efficient, and accountable

Strong sense of ownership and follow-through

Values long-term, professional working relationships built on trust and integrity



Application Instructions

Please include the words “Foot Traffic” in your application.  Please provide specific examples of accounts or businesses you have helped grow, including measurable results (e.g., increases in views, engagement, foot traffic, or sales), particularly for physical locations.

title: Cold callers
description:
We are a growing tutoring company looking for friendly, hardworking people to join our team as Cold Callers. You will be calling parents to introduce our tutoring services



We are looking for someone who is great on the phone. Your English needs to be very good—clear, fluent, and easy for parents to understand. This is very important because you will be speaking with busy parents who need to feel confident and comfortable right away.



We want someone kind and polite, but also persistent. 



You will be calling from a list of leads we provide. 



We pay $5 per hour for your time on the phone, plus $10 for every student who enrolls. You will need a quiet workspace, a reliable headset, and a stable internet connection. Evenings tend to work best for reaching parents, so flexibility with hours is a plus.



To apply, please send your resume and a short voice recording—about one or two minutes—introducing yourself and telling us why you would be good at talking to parents about tutoring.



We look forward to hearing from you.

title: Marketing- Social Media Content Developer & Video Editor
description:
About Us

Higher View Tax Services is not your average financial company. We’re a fast-growing, forward-thinking team that prioritizes excellence, connection, and creativity in everything we do. As we scale toward national goals, we’re looking for someone who can amplify our presence across key social media platforms and bring our brand voice to life with precision, strategy, and style.



Position Overview

We are hiring a creative and results-driven GHL Social Media Content Developer and Video Editor to join our marketing team. This role is responsible for creating scroll-stopping content, editing high-performing video assets, and managing content that drives engagement and conversions across
, Instagram, TikTok, and YouTube. This position also requires hands-on experience with GoHighLevel, including using the platform to manage content, run ads, and support marketing automation efforts.



This role is ideal for someone who understands trends, algorithms, branding, and performance marketing, and who can turn simple ideas into dynamic content that converts.



Key Responsibilities



Plan, create, edit, and schedule engaging content for
, Instagram, TikTok, and YouTube.



Edit short-form and long-form video content including reels, ads, YouTube Shorts, and educational videos.



Use GoHighLevel to support marketing efforts, including managing media assets, assisting with campaigns, and running ads through the platform.



Collaborate with internal tea
mbers to understand brand voice, goals, and messaging.



Develop and execute content and marketing strategies to increase engagement, leads, and followers.



Optimize posts and videos for reach, SEO, and conversions.



Stay current on social media platform updates, trends, and best practices.



Analyze performance metrics and provide insights and recommendations.



Requirements – Core Experience (Expert Level)



Minimum of 3 years of hands-on experience creating social media content for brands or businesses.



Proven portfolio of short-form and long-form video content (required).



Strong understanding of content strategy, not just video editing.



Ability to work independently, meet deadlines, and follow brand guidelines.



Comfortable receiving feedback and making quick revisions.



Demonstrated experience using GoHighLevel, including various platform functions and ad management.



Platforms and Social Media Expertise

Must have experience creating content optimized for Instagram (Reels, Stories, Carousel posts), TikTok, YouTube (Shorts and long-form), and
. Bonus experience includes LinkedIn and Pinterest. Must understand platform-specific aspect ratios, safe zones, trends, hooks, retention strategies, CTAs, caption structure, hashtag strategy, and basic analytics.



Video Editing and Design Tools (Required)

Expert-level proficiency in at least two of the following tools is required: Adobe Premiere Pro, Adobe After Effects, Adobe Photoshop, Adobe Illustrator, CapCut (desktop or mobile for professional use), Final Cut Pro, or Canva (professional, brand-driven use). Bonus tools include DaVinci Resolve, advanced Canva brand kits, and motion design tools.



Motion Graphics and Visual Skills

Must be able to create dynamic subtitles and captions, smooth transitions and cuts, zooms, overlays, callouts, simple motion graphics, and text animations. Must have a strong sense of pacing, storytelling, and visual hierarchy, and be able to match edits to a professional, bold, clean, and high-converting brand tone.



Content Types You Must Be Able to Produce

Short-form vertical videos, talking-head videos with captions, educational and explainer content, promotional and offer-based videos, testimonial and social proof content, trend-based content adapted to brand voice, branded graphics and thumbnails, and repurposed content from long-form videos into multiple short clips.



Audio and Video Quality Standards

Clean cuts, professional pacing, proper audio balancing, noise reduction, color correction, basic color grading, and exporting content in platform-optimized formats are required.



Workflow and Collaboration Tools

Experience using Google Drive or Dropbox for file management, project management tools such as Trello, Asana, or ClickUp, communication tools such as Slack or WhatsApp, and review tools like 
 (bonus). Ability to follow SOPs and naming conventions is required.



Bonus Experience (Highly Preferred but Not Required)

Experience working with U.S.-based brands, experience in finance, business, education, or service-based industries, understanding of funnels, CTAs, and conversion-focused content, and familiarity with social media scheduling tools such as Meta Business Suite or similar platforms.



Why Join Us

This is a fully remote role with flexible work structure. You’ll be part of a growing brand with national reach, have opportunities for growth and creative freedom, and work within a collaborative, supportive, and high-performance team culture.

title: Creative Strategist + Video Editor for Ads
description:

Different hooks, angles, and styles for testing

Hooks

Retention

Conversions

Ad structure

title: Instagram Appointment Setter
description:
Appointment Setter – Instagram (Remote)



Poa Landscape Design



About the Role



We’re looking for a confident, switched-on Appointment Setter to handle Instagram outreach, conversations, and lead qualification for our landscape design studio.



This is not a basic admin role.



You’ll be the first point of contact for potential clients, starting conversations, building trust, qualifying leads, and booking calls with people who are genuinely ready to invest in their garden.



What You’ll Be Doing

Sending cold Instagra
ssages to targeted prospects

Starting natural, engaging conversations (not robotic scripts)

Following up leads consistently and professionally

Qualifying prospects based on budget, timing, and project fit

Booking calls directly into our calendar

Managing conversations in DMs and keeping everything organised

Identifying high-quality opportunities and prioritising them

Who This Role is For

You’re confident starting conversations with strangers

You know how to guide a chat without being pushy

You can read people quickly and adjust your tone

You’re organised, consistent, and don’t drop leads

You enjoy sales, psychology, and communication

What We’re Looking For

Strong written communication, natural and conversational

Experience with Instagram outreach, DM sales, or appointment setting

Ability to handle objections and keep conversations moving

High attention to detail when qualifying leads

Reliable, responsive, and proactive

Bonus (But Not Essential)

Experience in high-ticket services (design, construction, property, etc.)

Understanding of Australian market or lifestyle

Experience using CRMs or tracking leads

What Success Looks Like

Consistent flow of qualified leads booked into calls

High response and engagement rates in DMs

Strong follow-up with minimal missed opportunities

Clear communication and smooth handover to the design team

About Us



Poa Landscape Design is a Melbourne-based studio creating modern, native gardens for busy professionals and families.



Our clients are investing $100K+ into their outdoor spaces, so every conversation matters. We focus on quality, not volume.



Pay Structure



(Open to discussion based on experience)



Base + performance incentives per closed project



How to Apply



Send through:



A short intro about yourself

Any experience with Instagram outreach or sales

Example messages or conversations you’ve had (if available)

To Stand Out



Start your application with:

“I know how to start conversations that convert.”

title: Customer Experience Representative
description:
Job Title: Customer Experience Representative

Rate: $3 up to $5/hour (based on experience)

Schedule: Must be available to work during the US (PST) time zone. 30 to 40 hours per week



We are seeking a Customer Experience Representative to join our dynamic team! This role is ideal for someone who excels at communication, learns quickly, and thrives in a fast-paced, customer-focused environment.



Challenge:

- As part of your application, please create a 60-second video or audio answering the following scenario:

"How would you defuse a disgruntled customer whose Estimated Time of Arrival (ETA) was late?"

- Your response should clearly describe your approach to handling the situation.

- Please do not exceed one minute, and do not include any personal or work experiences in your answer.



Requirements:

- Send a picture of your computer specs.

- Ability to work with a dual-monitor setup.



Preferred Qualifications:

- 1-2 years of experience in a BPO environment is an advantage.



How to Apply:

- Send your resume, the required video/audio, and your computer specs.

- Include the answer to this question in the
 subject line: What color do you get when you combine red and yellow?



Note: Only applicants who follow these instructions will be considered. Join us and grow your career with our amazing team!

title: Virtual Assistant (Admin + Bookkeeping + Light Social Media Support) Remote | Growth Opportunity

mbers

title: Bookkeeper (QuickBooks and Zero Specialist)
description:
Part-Time | Remote | £6 per hour



About the Role

We are recruiting on behalf of a growing UK-based accountancy firm, an 8-figure firm, seeking a detail-oriented and highly accurate Bookkeeper (QuickBooks Specialist) to support their client portfolio.



This is a key role responsible for maintaining clean, compliant, and up-to-date financial records across multiple clients. You will work closely with the Director to ensure bookkeeping is completed to a high standard, deadlines are met, and all records are fully prepared for accountant review.



Note: This is not basic data entry — this role requires ownership of bookkeeping, accuracy, and workflow.



Working Hours

10:00am – 3:00pm UK Time (Tuesday & Thursday)

5:00pm – 10:00pm Philippines Time



Key Responsibilities



Bookkeeping & QuickBooks Management

Maintain accurate, up-to-date records in QuickBooks Online

Post and allocate bank transactions

Manage purchase invoices and expenses

Post sales invoices and allocate receipts

Maintain clean and accurate ledgers (debtors, creditors, nominal)

Perform bank, credit card, and balance sheet reconciliations

Prepare VAT returns and support submissions

Process journal entries (within competence)

Ensure all records are fully supported and audit-ready



Deadlines & Workflow Management

Manage VAT, month-end, and reporting deadlines

Start work early and avoid last-minute deadlines

Request missing information proactively

Keep all work progressing without backlog



Client Communication

Respond to client queries within 1 working day

Communicate clearly and professionally

Keep communication simple and commercially aware

Escalate anything outside your level of expertise



Data Accuracy & Cleanliness

Maintain clean ledgers with no duplication or miscoding

Ensure all accounts are fully reconciled

Clear suspense accounts promptly

Monitor aged debtors and creditors

Regularly review bank feeds and financial data



Escalation & Awareness

Identify and flag unusual transactions

Highlight compliance risks or inconsistencies

Raise cash flow concerns where relevant

Escalate anything unclear or outside your scope



Reporting

Provide weekly updates on:



Work completed

Work in progress

Missing information

Risks or issues



Essential Skills & Experience

Strong experience using QuickBooks Online (non-negotiable)

Proven experience in bookkeeping or accountancy support

Strong understanding of reconciliations, VAT, and ledgers

High level of accuracy and attention to detail

Strong English communication skills

Able to manage workload independently

Organised and process-driven



What We’re Looking For

We want someone who:



Takes full ownership of their work

Keeps financial records clean, accurate, and review-ready

Works proactively (not reactively)

Manages deadlines without being chased

Communicates clearly with both clients and internal team

Spots issues early and raises them



What This Role Isn’t

Not basic data entry

Not leaving reconciliations unfinished

Not allowing backlog to build

Not ignoring errors or inconsistencies



How to Apply:

Use “Vixa Hire” in the subject line of your application.

Send your resume and cover letter.

Include your internet speed test result and a screenshot of your laptop specs.



If you’re a detail-driven QuickBooks specialist who takes pride in clean, accurate bookkeeping, this is a great opportunity to take ownership within a growing UK accountancy firm.

title: Sales Lead (Construction)
description:
Sales Representative — Electrical Construction Bid Operations



About the Role

You'll own the full bid lifecycle across two (and growing) electrical contractors — from submission through contract close. This is a phone-heavy, pipeline-driven role where your follow-up directly translates to revenue. Every bid gets tracked, every GC relationship gets worked, and nothing goes cold on your watch.



What You'll Do

Manage the live bid board daily. Every morning starts with reviewing what's pending, what needs follow-up, and what's close to winning.

Make 100+ outbound calls daily to GC project managers, estimators, and decision-makers. These are warm calls — we've already submitted a proposal. You're following up to learn where we stand, gauge competitor pricing, handle scope questions, and push toward award.

Run a disciplined follow-up cadence. Every bid gets touched at 3, 7, and 14 days. Every touchpoint is logged in the CRM with pricing intel, GC preferences, and timeline updates.

Conduct outreach to get our contractors on new bid lists. Cold and warm outreach to general contractors doing commercial retail and restaurant work in the LA market. Goal: more bid invitations every week.

Gather competitive intelligence. When a GC says "you were high" or "we went with someone else," you log it, spot pricing patterns, and flag where a small adjustment could flip a loss into a win.

Feed intel back to the estimating team. If you're hearing we're consistently high on a certain project type, you bring that data back so we can recalibrate before the next bid goes out.

Track win/loss metrics weekly: follow-up rate, response rate, deals moved to negotiation, contracts closed, and revenue attributed to your efforts.



Requirements

Strong English communication. You'll be calling American GCs who expect fluent, confident, real conversation — no scripts.

Sales or outbound calling experience (minimum 2 year). Inside sales, SDR/BDR, appointment setting, or BPO backgrounds all work.



Available during US Pacific Time hours. Most GC offices are reachable 7 AM–4 PM PT. You need meaningful overlap with that window.



CRM discipline. Every call logged, every status updated, no exceptions. If it's not in the system, it didn't happen.

Self-directed. You'll have daily targets but nobody hovering. You manage your own call list, prioritize the highest-value follow-ups, and flag problems before they become missed deals.



Comfortable with rejection. GCs are busy, often blunt, and sometimes don't return calls. Persistence without being pushy is the skill.



Coachable and curious. You'll be learning electrical construction terminology, project types, and GC relationship dynamics. The person who asks questions and absorbs fast will succeed here.



Strong Preference

Construction industry experience — especially electrical contracting, commercial buildouts, or tenant improvement work. If you've worked on the GC or sub side and understand how bids flow, scope gets negotiated, and awards get made, you'll ramp significantly faster and have an immediate edge.



Nice-to-Haves

Experience with bid platforms like BuildingConnected or PlanHub. Familiarity with CRM tools (
, HubSpot, or similar). Bilingual English/Spanish — valuable in the LA construction market. Any real estate or trades industry exposure.

title: Long-Form AI Video Editor + AI Storytelling Producer (Character Consistency)
description:
Type: Contract / Part-time (room to grow into full-time)



Remote: Yes



Start: ASAP



Tools: We provide the AI tools and accounts



(IMPORTANT: MUST SUBMIT A PORTFOLIO OF AI WORKS)



About the Role



We’re hiring a long-form AI video storyteller who can produce narrative-driven videos using AI generation tools and strong editing instincts. This role is NOT short-form reels—it’s for long-form storytelling (target 10–15 minute videos: YouTube-length narratives, episodic content, cinematic sequences).



You should be able to create videos that feel cohesive and intentional: consistent characters, consistent environments, consistent visual style, and strong pacing from start to finish.



We provide access to the AI tools. What we need from you is craft, taste, and storytelling and proven experience making AI-generated video content. We will provide all scripts for the projects.





What You’ll Do

- Produce long-form AI videos from concept to final export (with our direction).



- Maintain character consistency across scenes (face, body, wardrobe, style, mannerisms).



- Maintain visual consistency (lighting, color palette, camera language, environment continuity).



- Build scenes using AI tools and refine outputs until they match the story requirements.



- Assemble full edits: pacing, transitions, music, sound design, dialogue/VO, SFX.



- Work with our provided scripts and elevate them through visuals and editing choices.



- Create and manage a repeatable workflow for episodes (templates, style bibles, prompt systems).



- Organize assets and versions cleanly for handoffs and future iterations.





Key Skills We Need (Must-Have)



Long-form storytelling editing(structure, tension, payoffs, emotional rhythm).



Strong understanding of cinematic language (shots, continuity, pacing, coverage).



Proven ability to create and maintain:

- Consistent characters

-Consistent art direction

-Consistent locations / world-building



Experience with AI video generation pipelines and iteration workflows.



Clean workflow habits (file organization, naming, version control mindset).





AI Experience (What Your Portfolio Should Demonstrate)



We’re specifically looking for creators who already have AI projects showing:



Character continuity across multiple scenes.



Style consistency across an entire video (or episode).



Cohesive world-building (settings, props, clothing, time of day).



Ability to troubleshoot AI artifacts and keep quality high.



We do not require any specific AI toolssince we’ll provide the stack—what matters is that you understand the workflow and can execute.



Editing Tools



Premiere / DaVinci / After Effects (any is fine).



Strong audio skills are a big plus (dialogue clarity, music mixing, SFX layers).



Bonus: experience building pipelines (asset libraries, prompt libraries, shot lists).





What Success Looks Like



You can take a script/outline and deliver a cohesive long-form AI video.



Characters stay recognizable and stable from scene to scene.



Visuals feel like one connected world — not random AI clips stitched together.



The final video feels intentional, emotional, and watchable all the way through.

----------------------------------------------------

What to Send When Applying (Required)



Portfolio links (AI long-form preferred).



1–2 examples that specifically show character consistency across multiple scenes.



A short breakdown of your workflow (how you keep characters/visuals consistent).



Your rate (per minute of finished video OR per project) and weekly availability.



Bonus: Include a short “breakdown reel” showing prompts, iterations, and before/after improvements.





Nice-to-Haves



Episodic storytelling experience (series structure).



Shot listing / storyboarding ability.



Prompt systems / “style bible” creation.



Team collaboration (taking notes, iterating quickly, meeting deadlines).

 communication

title: Website Designer | Creative Portfolio Builder & Strategist
description:
Remote  |  Contract / Part-Time  | MINI Test Project to Start (use AI)



Type: Contract / Part-time (with potential to grow quickly within design branding team)

Location: Fully remote

Start: Paid test project if quality to level #2 candidate, then ongoing work if you crush it

Tools: Canva (required), AI tools (mandatory for design inspiration), Slack, Notion

Compensation: Salary with bonus (discussed after screening)

Team: 10+ Employees Across Canada/USA | 2+ VA's from Philippines | 5+ worldwide



>> Read this before you apply >  About Wovvo

Wovvo is a UGC platform built by two founders who got tired of watching talented creators struggle to find paid work. We’re bootstrapping in public, and we’re building the go-to destination for creators who want real brand deals, with a strong branded portfolio to go with it.



Part of our mission: helping creators present themselves like professionals on their Canva website (UGC portfolio). That’s where you come in.



>>  What You’ll Actually Do

You’ll be redesigning UGC creator portfolios from the ground up. Sometimes it’s re-vamp where the creator provides portfolio. Other times it’s from scratch. It’s not just making them look nice, but making them convert. Every portfolio you work on should answer one question for the brand looking at it: “Why should I hire this creator?”



>>  Day-to-day, that looks like:

Auditing existing creator portfolios and identifying what’s killing their conversion rate

Rebuilding portfolio structure, messaging, visual hierarchy, and credibility signals

Creating high-end portfolio designs in Canva that look custom, not 2016 style

Designing compelling case study sections, testimonial layouts, and video showcases

Using AI tools to speed up your workflow without sacrificing quality or originality

Use Canva features to make high-end quality website that reflects the creators personal brand

Turning around polished, brand-ready portfolios within tight timelines (target: ~7 hours per portfolio)



>>  This Role Is NOT For You If…

You think “good design” means picking a nice Canva template and swapping the text

You can’t explain WHY a design works, you just “feel” it

You’ve never thought about conversion, brand psychology, or what makes someone say yes

You need 3+ days to finish a single portfolio

You don’t use AI in your workflow (or don’t see the point)

You think “designer” and “strategist” are two different roles



>> This Role IS For You If…

You can look at a portfolio and immediately spot what’s holding it back

You understand that design is a tool for persuasion, not just aesthetics

You know how to make a portfolio look “high-end” and can articulate exactly what that means

You’re fast, structured, and can manage your own workflow without hand-holding

You use AI to accelerate your process, not replace your thinking

You’re genuinely excited about the creator economy and helping people land brand deals



>> Must-haves:

Advanced Canva skills (not beginner, not “I use templates” actually advanced)

Strong visual design eye:  typography, spacing, hierarchy, color

Portfolio or case study experience (show us work, not just a resume)

Understanding of conversion design:  how layout and messaging drive action

Experience with AI design tools (Midjourney, ChatGPT, or similar) as part of your process

Ability to work fast without sacrificing quality (~7 hour turnaround per portfolio)



>>  Bonus points:

Experience in the UGC or creator economy space

Understanding of what brands and agencies actually look for when hiring creators

Background in marketing, branding, or landing page design



----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------



How We Evaluate

We’re scoring on 6 dimensions. Design ability is only one of them.





>> WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

Design Thinking: Can you diagnose problems and prioritize fixes, not just make things look nice?



Conversion Awareness: Do you understand why a brand says yes or no? Do your changes drive action?



Creativity & Originality: Are your ideas fresh, or are they the same suggestions everyone gives?



Brand Psychology: Do you understand how credibility, trust, and positioning work visually?



Structure & Balance: Can you organize information so it flows logically and looks intentional?



Strategic Thinking: Do you think like a strategist who designs, or a designer who guesses?



>> Screening Questions
